message:
Wire connector stub types to module namespace

Replace placeholder comments in all six connector module files with
pub use declarations re-exporting the stub connector types generated
by stub_connector! macro in connectors/mod.rs.

Module structure rationale:
Each connector lives in a dedicated module file (codex.rs, cline.rs,
gemini.rs, claude_code.rs, opencode.rs, amp.rs) to provide namespace
organization and clear separation when real implementations are added.
For example, Codex connector will eventually contain:
- CodexConnector struct with detection logic checking for $CODEX_HOME
- Private helper functions for JSONL parsing (parse_rollout_event,
  reconstruct_session_from_events, extract_workspace_from_metadata)
- Format-specific types (RolloutEvent enum, SessionMetadata struct)
- Tests module with sample JSONL fixtures

Current stub implementation:
All six modules simply re-export the macro-generated stub types from
parent mod.rs, enabling:
- Qualified imports in application code: use connectors::codex::CodexConnector
  rather than bare use connectors::CodexConnector (clearer origin)
- Consistent API surface for indexer orchestration layer: indexer can
  instantiate CodexConnector::new(), call .detect() and .scan() even
  before real parsing logic exists (returns empty results but compiles)
- Incremental development: each connector can be implemented independently
  without breaking builds—stub returns empty Vec, real implementation
  returns parsed conversations, indexer doesn't need to know which is
  which

Connector implementation order (per PLAN § 14):
1. Codex (PLAN § 14.4): Simplest format, JSONL event stream with clear
   session boundaries
2. Cline (PLAN § 14.5): VS Code globalStorage with well-documented JSON
   structure from StackOverflow/GitHub issues
3. Gemini (PLAN § 14.6): JSON checkpoints/logs with known location from
   gemini-cli prettifier script
4. Claude Code (PLAN § 14.9): JSONL similar to Codex but less documented,
   need to reverse-engineer from ~/.claude/projects samples
5. OpenCode (PLAN § 14.9): SQLite schema introspection, straightforward
   SELECT queries but need to locate .opencode directories
6. Amp (PLAN § 14.9): Most complex, partial cache with underdocumented
   format, exploratory scanning required

File-by-file changes:
- codex.rs: pub use crate::connectors::CodexConnector (was "//! codex
  connector placeholder.")
- claude_code.rs: pub use crate::connectors::ClaudeCodeConnector
- gemini.rs: pub use crate::connectors::GeminiConnector
- cline.rs: pub use crate::connectors::ClineConnector
- opencode.rs: pub use crate::connectors::OpenCodeConnector
- amp.rs: pub use crate::connectors::AmpConnector

All six files now expose identical API (Connector trait implementation)
via their respective types, ready for indexer integration in run_index()
function (future commit) where each connector will be instantiated,
detected, and scanned in a loop over enabled agents from config.

This establishes module skeleton per Rust conventions (one module per
file, clear re-export boundaries) before adding implementation details
that will differentiate each connector's internal structure. Tests will
also live in these module files (codex::tests, cline::tests) rather
than centralized tests/ directory to keep connector logic and test
fixtures colocated.

Related: PLAN_TO_MAKE_CODING_AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH.md § 7 (connector
specs), § 14 (implementation order), connectors/mod.rs (trait + macro),
README.md (connectors/ module overview).
